title: carex bipartita var. scaber
intro: Carex bipartita var. scaber, also known as rough-fruited sedge, is a plant species belonging to the family Cyperaceae, characterized by its distinctive, scabrous fruit and bipartite leaf structure. Traditionally, Carex bipartita var. scaber has been utilized in folk medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The plant's health benefits are said to include relief from fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve kidney function. In herbal preparations, Carex bipartita var. scaber is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically validated.
